title: Bateshwar Hindu temples
tagline: Bateshwar Hindu temples — spiritual town of Madhya Pradesh

overview.short: The Bateshwar Hindu temples (romanised: baṭeśvar; /bəʈeːɕvər/) are a group of nearly 200 sandstone Hindu temples and their ruins in north Madhya Pradesh in post-Gupta, early Gurjar
overview.description: The Bateshwar Hindu temples (romanised: baṭeśvar; /bəʈeːɕvər/) are a group of nearly 200 sandstone Hindu temples and their ruins in north Madhya Pradesh in post-Gupta, early Gurjara-Pratihara style of North Indian temple architecture. It is about 35 kilometres (22 mi) north of Gwalior and about 30 kilometres (19 mi) east of Morena town. The temples are mostly small and spread over about 25 acres (10 ha) site. They are dedicated to Shiva, Vishnu and Shakti – representing the three major traditions within Hinduism.

topPlaces.name: Chausath Yogini Temple, Mitaoli
topPlaces.description: The Chausath Yogini Temple, Mitaoli, also known as Ekattarso Mahadeva Temple, is an 11th-century temple in Morena district in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh. Build during Kachchhapaghata reign, it is one of the well-preserved Yogini temples in India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bateshwar Hindu temples?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bateshwar Hindu temples is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bateshwar Hindu temples?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Gwalior Airport (15 km) and the nearest railway station is Gwalior Junction (23 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bateshwar Hindu temples?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bateshwar Hindu temples at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bateshwar Hindu temples?
faq.a: Stays in Bateshwar Hindu temples start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bateshwar Hindu temples?
faq.a: The top attractions are Chausath Yogini Temple, Mitaoli.
